meta.sprintTitle: Admin Console v1 - Billing + Plan Support View
meta.goal: Enable authorised admins to inspect billing, subscription, checkout, and plan state for users from the Admin Console so support can diagnose access, upgrade, sandbox, and payment-related issues without using payment-provider dashboards directly.

basedOnPriorSprints: Sprint 40 - Early Alpha / sandbox upgrade messaging
GA4 funnel and lead capture planning
whyNow: Users will raise issues such as paid but not unlocked, sandbox confusion, failed checkout, and missing premium export access
Support needs read-first billing visibility before public launch and paid flows expand
Billing state must be understandable alongside user/project/export state in one admin system

nonGoals: Changing prices or products from admin console
Subscription cancellation on behalf of user
dependencies: Sprint 45 admin auth and RBAC foundation
Existing payment provider integration such as Stripe or equivalent
Billing/subscription records in app database or provider sync layer

operational: Support admin can identify a user's plan and subscription state within a few seconds
Support admin can diagnose common access issues such as failed checkout, expired trial, inactive subscription, or sandbox/live mismatch
Admins no longer need to use provider dashboard for most first-line billing support cases
security: 0 unauthorised users can access admin billing data
0 roles without billing permission can access billing views
Sensitive financial data is masked or excluded by default
quality: 100% of billing admin endpoints are permission-guarded server-side
Billing state mapping is consistent between UI and backend
Audit logs exist for billing detail access and manual support actions

personas.name: Super Admin
personas.description: Can view all billing state, payment events summary, and perform allowed support actions such as temporary plan overrides.

personas.name: Support Admin
personas.description: Can inspect billing/subscription state and resolve common access issues using safe internal controls.

personas.name: Normal App User
personas.description: Regular Memorioso customer. Must never access billing admin tooling.

inScope: Billing summary on admin user detail page
View current plan, entitlement, and subscription status
View latest checkout and payment attempt summaries
View sandbox vs live mode status
View billing-related support events and failure reasons
Optional safe manual actions such as temporary access grant or resync billing state
Audit logging for billing views and support actions

architecture.recommendedApproach: Expose a support-safe billing read model by combining internal plan entitlement data with synced payment provider data. Keep provider-specific raw objects out of the UI. Add a small set of guarded support actions only if needed for operational recovery.
principles: Show app-level entitlement alongside provider-level subscription state
Server-side permission enforcement for all views and actions

rules: billing.view is required for any billing page or API
billing.support_action is required for actions like temporary entitlement grant or billing resync
support.view may be required for support-sensitive event/failure summaries
Client-side visibility must mirror, not replace, server-side checks

sourceEntities.name: subscriptions
sourceEntities.purpose: App-level subscription state and plan linkage

sourceEntities.name: billing_customers
sourceEntities.purpose: Mapping between app user and payment provider customer

sourceEntities.name: checkout_sessions_or_payment_attempts
sourceEntities.purpose: Track upgrade attempts and checkout outcomes

sourceEntities.name: plan_entitlements
sourceEntities.purpose: Actual app access state driven by billing

sourceEntities.name: admin_audit_logs
sourceEntities.purpose: Trace billing views and support actions

supportCasesToSolve.case: User paid but premium/export access not unlocked
User is confused by sandbox mode
Subscription cancelled but app still unlocked

userStories.title: Inspect billing state for a user
userStories.iWant: to see plan, subscription, and entitlement state together
userStories.soThat: I can understand whether the user's access should be active

userStories.title: Diagnose checkout failure
userStories.iWant: to see the latest checkout and payment attempt summaries
userStories.soThat: I can explain what happened and next steps

userStories.title: See sandbox versus live state
userStories.iWant: clear indication of test or live billing mode
userStories.soThat: I can avoid confusion during early alpha

userStories.title: Apply safe support recovery action
userStories.iWant: to trigger a limited billing support action
userStories.soThat: I can restore user access in controlled cases without engineering help

userStories.title: Prevent billing access for unrelated admin roles
userStories.iWant: content admins blocked from billing data

functionalRequirements.title: Billing panel on user detail
requirements: Enhance /admin/users/:userId with a billing summary section or link to dedicated billing page
Show current plan, entitlement status, subscription status, and billing mode
Show latest relevant billing timestamps where available

functionalRequirements.title: Dedicated user billing detail view
requirements: Provide /admin/users/:userId/billing page
Show plan summary, entitlement summary, subscription summary, and recent billing support events
Show latest checkout/payment attempt summary in support-friendly language
Show sandbox/live indicator prominently

functionalRequirements.title: Subscription detail drill-down
requirements: Provide /admin/billing/subscriptions/:subscriptionId page or equivalent panel
Show provider-mapped status, current period timing, latest invoice/payment summary if available
Keep raw provider payload hidden from normal support UI

functionalRequirements.title: Safe support actions
requirements: Allow only explicitly approved actions such as billing resync and temporary manual entitlement grant
Each action must be permission-guarded by billing.support_action
Each action must require confirmation and capture reason/note
Each action must be audit logged
Actions must be limited in scope and reversible where feasible

functionalRequirements.title: Support-friendly diagnosis
requirements: Map technical provider states to human-readable support labels
Display likely issue diagnosis such as payment succeeded but entitlement stale, checkout incomplete, or trial expired
Show recommended next step where possible

functionalRequirements.title: Permission-aware data exposure
requirements: Only roles with billing.view can access billing pages and APIs
Content admin must not see billing links, sections, or APIs
Sensitive identifiers must be masked unless full value is operationally necessary

functionalRequirements.title: Read-first and minimal action design
requirements: Billing console should primarily support diagnosis
Do not include refund, cancel, or destructive provider controls
Support actions must not modify pricing or provider product configuration

functionalRequirements.title: Audit logging
requirements: Log access to billing detail pages
Log manual support actions with actor, target user/subscription, reason, and timestamp
Log action success/failure outcome

pages.title: User Billing
pages.purpose: Inspect a specific user's plan, subscription, entitlement, and checkout/payment summaries.

pages.title: Subscription Detail
pages.purpose: Inspect a specific subscription state in support-safe format.

uxRules: Use strong visual distinction between sandbox and live mode
Prioritise current entitlement and current subscription state at the top
Show support diagnosis in plain language
Any support action must require explicit confirmation and note

apiSurface.note: Adapt names to your existing backend conventions and billing provider contracts.

endpoints.purpose: Return admin billing detail for a user.
Trigger billing/provider sync for a user.
Grant or extend temporary access under controlled support rules.

error.message: You do not have permission to perform this billing support action.

allowedActions.description: Refresh billing/subscription state from provider or internal sync source.
Grant temporary access for a defined period to resolve support issues.

rules: Every manual action must capture admin reason and note
Every manual action must be audit logged
Temporary entitlement should have expiry where possible
Manual action must not mutate provider-side pricing/catalog
Manual action must not silently override active billing state without visible trace

restrictedExamples: full provider raw payload by default
maskingRules: Provider IDs should be masked in most list/summary views
Do not expose card fingerprints, client secrets, or webhook payloads
Use support-safe summaries rather than raw JSON from provider

technicalTasks.title: Define billing admin read models
technicalTasks.description: Create support-safe DTOs/view models for user billing and subscription detail responses.

technicalTasks.title: Build user billing detail API
technicalTasks.description: Combine internal entitlement state with provider-synced subscription/checkout/payment summaries.

technicalTasks.title: Build subscription detail API
technicalTasks.description: Return support-safe detail for a specific subscription record.

technicalTasks.title: Implement billing support actions
technicalTasks.description: Add guarded resync and temporary manual entitlement actions with confirmation, validation, and audit logging.

technicalTasks.title: Build billing UI
technicalTasks.description: Create billing detail page and integrate summary block into user detail flow.

technicalTasks.title: Build subscription detail UI
technicalTasks.description: Create support-safe subscription page/panel for drill-down inspection.

technicalTasks.title: Implement diagnosis mapping
technicalTasks.description: Map internal/provider combinations to support-friendly diagnosis messages and next steps.

technicalTasks.title: Audit logging integration
technicalTasks.description: Log billing page access and all manual billing support actions.

technicalTasks.title: Testing
technicalTasks.description: Add unit, integration, and E2E coverage for billing support flows.

diagnosisRulesExamples.diagnosis: Payment appears successful, but app access is not aligned. A billing resync or temporary entitlement may be needed.

when: latest payment status = pending or failed
diagnosisRulesExamples.diagnosis: Checkout did not complete successfully. The user may need to retry payment.
Trial access has ended and no active paid subscription is present.
This account is operating in sandbox/test mode. No real payment should be expected.

loggingNotes: Do not log raw payment provider secrets or customer payment details
Keep audit logs focused on actor, target, action, reason, and timestamp
Use masked identifiers in general application logs

securityRequirements: All billing admin endpoints must be protected by admin auth and billing-specific permissions
Content admin must not access billing UI or APIs
Manual support actions must require explicit permission, confirmation, and note
Billing pages remain noindex and excluded from sitemap
Unauthorised actors must not infer whether a billing record or subscription exists
Provider-specific secrets and sensitive payment data must never be returned to client

acceptanceCriteria.when: they open a user's billing page
acceptanceCriteria.then: they can see current plan, entitlement, subscription status, billing mode, and latest billing summaries
the admin sees a support-friendly diagnosis and latest attempt summary

acceptanceCriteria.when: they attempt to access billing routes or APIs
they trigger billing resync or temporary entitlement grant
acceptanceCriteria.then: the action is validated, confirmed, executed, and audit logged
the system records reason, note, actor, and expiry if configured

acceptanceCriteria.when: they call any billing admin endpoint
acceptanceCriteria.then: the request is rejected without leaking billing record existence

unitTests: diagnosis rules map billing/subscription/entitlement states correctly
masking serializer excludes restricted payment data
manual support action policy blocks unsupported actions
integrationTests: content_admin is blocked from all billing routes
e2eTests: support_admin opens user billing page and sees support summary
support_admin sees sandbox mode banner when applicable
support_admin performs billing resync with confirmation and note
negativeTests: unauthorised actor cannot infer whether subscription exists
manual entitlement without reason is rejected
attempt to perform blocked billing action is rejected
raw provider payload is never exposed in client response

risks.risk: Billing UI exposes too much sensitive provider data
risks.mitigation: Use explicit allow-list serializers and masked identifiers

risks.risk: App entitlement and provider subscription state diverge
risks.mitigation: Show both states side-by-side and add resync workflow plus last sync timestamp

risks.risk: Support actions become an uncontrolled override mechanism
risks.mitigation: Limit actions, require reason/note, set expiry, and audit everything

risks.risk: Sandbox/live confusion persists in early alpha
risks.mitigation: Make mode explicit in admin and user-facing billing flows

recommendedOrder: Define billing read models and exposure rules
futureSprintHooks: Sprint 49: Feedback, moderation, and support inbox
Sprint 50: Feature flags and operational controls
Future: billing analytics and conversion dashboard
Future: refund/request workflows with stronger approval model

definitionOfDone: User billing page exists with plan, entitlement, subscription, and mode visibility
Subscription detail drill-down exists in support-safe format
Content admins are blocked from billing access
Optional support actions are limited, confirmed, validated, and audit logged
Sensitive payment/provider data is masked or excluded
Automated tests cover happy paths, denial paths, and support actions
